FT_IC1.evidence: U.S. ABCD Study, Year 2 (2018-2020), ages 11-12, N=10,157, diverse national cohort

FT_IC5.evidence: Original empirical analysis; multivariable logistic regression with sociodemographic predictors of online dating

FT_IC6.evidence: Peer-reviewed published article in BMC Research Notes

FT_IC2.evidence: Online dating app use (IV) paired only with sociodemographic outcomes (sex, age, race/ethnicity, household income, parental education, sexual orientation). No individual-trait-level outcome (neural, behavioral, health, cognitive, well-being) examined. Sociodemographic-only on outcome side fails FT-IC2

notes: Paper examines prevalence and correlates of online dating but outcome variables are purely demographic, not individual-trait-level constructs

FT_IC1.evidence: Perspective/commentary article, not original ABCD data analysis. Cites ABCD findings narratively but does not analyze ABCD data directly

FT_IC5.evidence: Perspective article providing clinical recommendations and review; not original empirical analysis of ABCD data

notes: Commentary on smartphone reduction strategies; mentions ABCD findings but is not an empirical analysis paper

FT_IC1.evidence: U.S. ABCD Study, 2-year follow-up (2019-2020), N=10,147, Mage=12.0, 48% female, 20% Black, 20% Hispanic, diverse national sample

FT_IC5.evidence: Original empirical analysis; exposome-wide association study (ExWAS) with linear-mixed-effects models and validation in independent testing subsample

FT_IC6.evidence: Peer-reviewed published article in NPP - Digital Psychiatry and Neuroscience (Nature portfolio)

FT_IC2.evidence: Social media use operationalized as IV: weekday/weekend screentime, app usage (TikTok, Instagram, Snapchat, etc.), cyberbullying exposure, secret accounts, addictive use patterns. Paired with individual-trait-level DV: mental health (Brief Problems Monitor Total Problems T-scores, suicide attempt history). Qualifies conceptually as SM-specific exposure

FT_IC3.evidence: Multiple numeric quantitative results: linear-mixed-effects coefficient (estimate=1.78, 95% CI [1.58-1.98], t=17.41, p<0.001) for digital exposomic risk and BPM T-scores; logistic regression OR=1.76 (95% CI [1.39-2.23], p<0.001) for suicide attempts; R² explained variance=15.61%

FT_IC4.evidence: Same as FT-IC3; quantitative results reported for SM exposure and mental health association

FT_EC7_reason: Design is cross-sectional (2019-2020 ABCD baseline assessment), not longitudinal. EC7 exclusion applies only to longitudinal designs with SM-as-DV-only. Cross-sectional exempt from EC7
notes: Data-driven exposome approach aggregating multiple SM exposures into composite risk scores; SM appears as IV throughout; extensive sensitivity analyses confirm robustness
